# Build & Development Tools (developer machine requirements)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# These are tools needed to BUILD and DEVELOP the project.
# End users do not need these — only the compiled .exe output.

# ── PC-SIDE BUILD ────────────────────────────────────────────
Python 3.11+
  Download: https://www.python.org/downloads/
  Notes:    Add to PATH during install. Use 64-bit.

pip (bundled with Python)
  Usage: Install all packages from python_packages.txt

PyInstaller 6.x
  Install: pip install pyinstaller
  Usage:   Compiles the Python project into a standalone .exe with all deps bundled
  Notes:   Use --onedir mode (not --onefile) for better AV compatibility and faster startup
  Alt:     Nuitka (produces faster runtime but longer compile — optional future optimization)

Git
  Download: https://git-scm.com/download/win
  Usage:    Version control, submodules for any vendored OSS libs

PowerShell 7+
  Download: https://github.com/PowerShell/PowerShell
  Usage:    Build scripts, setup automation, Windows-specific tasks

Windows Subsystem for Linux 2 (WSL2) + Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
  Setup:    wsl --install (Windows 11 built-in)
  Usage:    ONLY if specific Linux-only tooling is needed (e.g. certain libimobiledevice
            build steps, Linux-only crypto tools). Treat as last resort.

Visual Studio Build Tools 2022 (C++ workload)
  Download: https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/downloads/#build-tools-for-visual-studio-2022
  Usage:    Required to compile any Python packages with C extensions (pycryptodome,
            pyusb, pysqlcipher3) on Windows

# ── ANDROID BUILD ────────────────────────────────────────────
Android Studio (Hedgehog 2023.1.1 or newer)
  Download: https://developer.android.com/studio
  Usage:    IDE for companion APK development and build

Android SDK
  API Level 26 minimum (Android 8.0) — compile and min target
  API Level 35 (Android 15) — target SDK
  Install via Android Studio SDK Manager

Android SDK Build Tools
  Version: 34.0.0+
  Install via Android Studio SDK Manager

Android SDK Platform Tools
  Contains: adb, fastboot
  Install via Android Studio SDK Manager (also bundled separately in /bin)

Java JDK 17 (LTS)
  Download: https://adoptium.net/ (Eclipse Temurin recommended)
  Notes:    Android Studio ships its own JDK — external JDK only needed for
            command-line Gradle builds
  Set JAVA_HOME to JDK 17 path

Gradle (wrapper bundled in companion_app project)
  Version:  8.x (via gradlew wrapper — no manual install needed)
  Usage:    companion_app build system

Kotlin
  Version:  1.9+ (bundled with Android Studio / Gradle plugin — no manual install)

# ── OPTIONAL / QUALITY OF LIFE ───────────────────────────────
VS Code
  Usage:    Recommended editor for Python modules (with Pylance, Black formatter)
  Extensions: Python, Pylance, GitLens, Even Better TOML

Black (Python formatter)
  Install: pip install black
  Usage:   Code style consistency across all .py modules

Pylint / Flake8
  Install: pip install pylint flake8
  Usage:   Lint Python modules before commit

DB Browser for SQLite
  Download: https://sqlitebrowser.org/
  Usage:    Inspect iOS backup SQLite files, Android databases during development

iExplorer / iMazing (trial)
  Usage:    Reference tool — verify extraction results during development
  Note:     Free tier is sufficient for spot-checking during dev

Wireshark + usbpcap
  Usage:    Inspect iOS USB protocol traffic for reverse engineering backup/AFC behavior
  Download: https://www.wireshark.org/
